name: pxl
documentation:
# PXL

A collection of bits of Python code I've found useful for working with data,
plotting, etc., which aren't (to my knowledge) part of NumPy, Scipy, pandas, or
matplotlib.

## Installation

Run

    pip install pxl

modules.name: pxl.tests.test_styleplot
documentation: Tests for the pxl.styleplot module.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: pxl.tests.test_styleplot.test_set_sns
documentation: Test the Seaborn plot styling.

functions.name: pxl.tests.test_fdiff.test_second_order_diff
documentation: Test `second_order_diff`.

functions.name: pxl.tests.test_fdiff.test_second_order_diff_uncertainties
documentation: Test that `second_order_diff` works with uncertainties.

functions.name: pxl.tests.test_timeseries.test_spectrum_band_averaging
documentation: Test band averaging with `psd` function.

functions.name: pxl.tests.test_timeseries.test_average_over_area_uncertainties
documentation: Test `average_over_area` with uncertainties.

functions.name: pxl.tests.test_timeseries.test_combine_std
documentation: Test `timeseries.combine_std`.

modules.name: pxl.io
documentation: This is a collection of useful I/O functions.

functions.name: pxl.io.savejson
documentation:
Save data from a dictionary in JSON format. Note that this only
works to the second level of the dictionary with Numpy arrays.

functions.name: pxl.io.loadjson
documentation:
Load data from text file in JSON format.

Numpy arrays are converted if specified with the `asnparrays` keyword
argument. Note that this only works to the second level of the dictionary.
Returns a single dict.

functions.name: pxl.io.savecsv
documentation: Save a dictionary of data to CSV.

functions.name: pxl.io.loadcsv
documentation:
Load data from CSV file.

Returns a single dict with column names as keys.

functions.name: pxl.io.savehdf
documentation:
Save a dictionary of arrays to file--similar to how `scipy.io.savemat`
works. If `datadict` is a DataFrame, it will be converted automatically.

functions.name: pxl.io.loadhdf
documentation:
Load all data from top level of HDF5 file--similar to how
`scipy.io.loadmat` works.

functions.name: pxl.io.save_hdf_metadata
documentation: Save a dictionary of metadata to a group's attrs.

functions.name: pxl.io.load_hdf_metadata
documentation: Load attrs of the desired group into a dictionary.

modules.name: pxl.styleplot
documentation: This module contains helper functions for working with matplotlib.

functions.name: pxl.styleplot.set_sns
documentation:
Set default plot style using seaborn.

Font size is set to match the size of the tick labels, rather than the axes
labels.

functions.name: pxl.styleplot.label_subplot
documentation: Create a subplot label.

modules.name: pxl.timeseries
documentation: A collection of useful time series analysis functions.

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.sigmafilter
documentation: Remove datapoints outside of a specified standard deviation range.

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.psd
documentation:
Compute one-sided power spectral density, subtracting mean automatically.

Parameters
----------
t : Time array
data : Time series data
window : {None, "Hanning"}
n_band_average : Number of samples over which to band average

Returns
-------
f : Frequency array
psd : Spectral density array

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.runningstd
documentation:
Compute the running standard deviation of a time series.

Returns `t_new`, `std_r`.

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.smooth
documentation: Smooth data with a moving average.

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.calcstats
documentation:
Calculate the mean and standard deviation of some array between
t1 and t2 provided the sample rate sr.

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.average_over_area
documentation:
Averages a quantity `q` over a rectangular area given a 2D array and
the x and y vectors for sample locations, using the trapezoidal rule

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.build_plane_arrays
documentation:
Build a 2-D array out of data taken in the same plane, for contour
plotting.

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.corr_coeff
documentation: Compute lagged correlation coefficient for two time series.

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.autocorr_coeff
documentation: Calculate the autocorrelation coefficient.

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.integral_scale
documentation:
Calculate the integral scale of a time series by integrating up to
the first zero crossing.

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.combine_std
documentation:
Compute combined standard deviation for subsets.

See https://stats.stackexchange.com/questions/43159/    how-to-calculate-pooled-variance-of-two-groups-given-known-group-variances-    mean for derivation.

Parameters
----------
n : numpy array of sample sizes
mean : numpy array of sample means
std : numpy array of sample standard deviations

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.calc_multi_exp_unc
documentation:
Calculate expanded uncertainty using values from multiple runs.

Note that this function assumes the statistic is a mean value, therefore
the combined standard deviation is divided by `sqrt(N)`.

Parameters
----------
sys_unc : numpy array of systematic uncertainties
n : numpy array of numbers of samples per set
std : numpy array of sample standard deviations
dof : numpy array of degrees of freedom
confidence : Confidence interval for t-statistic

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.student_t
documentation: Return Student-t statistic for given DOF and confidence interval.

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.calc_uncertainty
documentation: Calculate the combined standard uncertainty of a quantity.

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.calc_exp_uncertainty
documentation:
Calculate expanded uncertainty.

Parameters
----------
n : Number of independent samples
std : Sample standard deviation
sys_unc : Systematic uncertainty (b in Coleman and Steele)
rel_unc : Relative uncertainty of each systematic error source (guess 0.25)
confidence : Confidence interval (0, 1)
mean : bool whether or not the quantity is a mean value

Returns
-------
exp_unc : Expanded uncertainty
nu_x : Degrees of freedom

functions.name: pxl.timeseries.find_amp_phase
documentation:
Estimate amplitude and phase of an approximately sinusoidal quantity
using `scipy.optimize.curve_fit`.

Phase is defined as the angle at which the cosine curve fit reaches its
first peak. It is assumed that phase is positive. For example:

    data_fit = amp*np.cos(npeaks*(angle - phase)) + mean_data

Parameters
----------
angle : numpy array
    Time series of angle values in radians
data : numpy array
    Time series of data to be fit
npeaks : int
    Number of peaks per revolution, or normalized frequency
min_phase : float
    Minimum phase to allow for guess to least squares fit

Returns
-------
amp : float
    Amplitude of regressed cosine
phase : float
    Angle of the first peak in radians

modules.name: pxl.fdiff
documentation: Finite difference utilities.

functions.name: pxl.fdiff.second_order_diff
documentation:
Compute second order difference of an array.

A 2nd order forward difference is used for the first point, 2nd order
central difference for interior, and 2nd order backward difference for last
point, returning an array the same length as the input array.

This probably doesn't need to exist since NumPy has a `gradient`
function.
